title: Greek Alphabet Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the Greek alphabet with this comprehensive quiz.

questions.question: What is the first letter of the Greek alphabet?
questions.explanation: Α (Alpha) is the first letter of the Greek alphabet, similar to English 'A'.

questions.question: What is the last letter of the Greek alphabet?
questions.explanation: Ω (Omega) is the last letter of the Greek alphabet, similar to English 'O'.

questions.question: How many letters are in the Greek alphabet?
questions.explanation: The Greek alphabet has exactly 24 letters, from Alpha to Omega.

questions.question: Which Greek letter makes the 'th' sound as in 'think'?
questions.explanation: Θ (Theta) makes the 'th' sound as in 'think' or 'theta'.

questions.question: What is the name of the Greek letter Β?
questions.explanation: Β is called Beta, similar to English 'B'.

questions.question: Which Greek letter is pronounced like 'g' in 'go'?
questions.explanation: Γ (Gamma) is pronounced like 'g' in 'go' or 'gamma'.

questions.question: What is the name of the Greek letter that looks like a circle?
questions.explanation: Ο (Omicron) is the small circle, while Ω (Omega) is the large circle with a line underneath.

questions.question: Which Greek letter makes the 'ph' sound as in 'phone'?
questions.explanation: Φ (Phi) makes the 'ph' sound as in 'phone' or 'phi'.

questions.question: What is the name of the Greek letter that looks like a cross?
questions.explanation: Χ (Chi) looks like a cross and is pronounced like 'ch' in 'loch'.

questions.question: Which Greek letter is pronounced like 's' in 'sun'?
questions.explanation: Σ (Sigma) is pronounced like 's' in 'sun' or 'sigma'.

instructions: Answer all questions about the Greek alphabet. You have 15 minutes to complete this quiz. Good luck!

metadata.author: Dr. Sarah Johnson
learningObjectives: Understand the relationship between Greek and English letters
